item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Climate Disaster Resilience Funding
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Climate Disaster Resilience Funding

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Climate disaster preparation and resilience funding in the 119th Congress addressing the documented increasing frequency and severity of climate-driven disasters (hurricanes, wildfires, floods, heat events).

What does it actually do?

  • FEMA pre-disaster mitigation — increased funding for hardening against future disasters
  • National Flood Insurance Program — modifications to actuarial-soundness and affordability balance
  • Wildfire risk reduction — funding for fuels-management on federal land + community-protection grants
  • Heat resilience — funding for cooling centers, energy assistance, urban tree canopy
  • Tribal climate adaptation — separate funding stream for Tribal climate-adaptation projects

Who votes when?

  • House Transportation & Infrastructure + Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Climate disasters disproportionately affect cooperative-class members (manufactured housing, rental housing, lower-income communities). Composes with Save-the-World Paper 3 (Universal Cooperative Shelter), Save-the-World Paper 5 (Earth Stewardship), and #8 Defense Klaus (crisis-response architecture).

item.title: I'm Just a Bill: Disaster SNAP Emergency Authorization
item.description:
👀 I’m Just a Bill — Disaster SNAP Emergency Authorization

A Liana Banyan “I’m Just a Bill” Bounty Poster

What is this bill?

Disaster-SNAP (D-SNAP) program expansion in the 119th Congress. D-SNAP provides temporary food assistance to households affected by federally-declared disasters; current proposals expand eligibility, streamline application, and increase per-household amounts.

What does it actually do?

  • Streamlined eligibility — automatic D-SNAP enrollment for current SNAP recipients in disaster areas
  • Application simplification — single-form application; mobile-accessible
  • Benefit duration — extends D-SNAP duration for prolonged-impact disasters
  • Hot foods — explicitly allows D-SNAP for hot-prepared foods in disaster areas (often the only food available)
  • Coordination with disaster relief — composes with FEMA Individual Assistance

Who votes when?

  • House Agriculture + Senate Agriculture active consideration

How does this affect cooperative-class families?

Composes directly with #1 Lets Make Dinner Freezer Host network (cooperative-class food infrastructure for members in disaster-affected areas) and #2 Lets Get Groceries local supply chain. Disaster-SNAP modernization makes the federal floor work better for cooperative-class members in crisis.
